name: Compile a daily digest with Airtable and Telegram with instant alerts

parameters.text:
=Summarize these KPIs for leadership in 5 bullet points:
{{ JSON.stringify($json) }}
=✅ Compile a daily digest with Airtable and Telegram with instant alerts — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 8:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Gather from Airtable

### Gather from Airtable

Searches Airtable's 'Events' table by formula (`={LastModified} > DATEADD(NOW(), -1, 'days')`) for records the pipeline hasn't handled yet.

Ports: ①→ Blend Sources · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Gather from Airtable 2

Searches Airtable's 'Events' table by formula (`={LastModified} > DATEADD(NOW(), -1, 'days')`) for records the pipeline hasn't handled yet.

Ports: ①→ Blend Sources · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Blend Sources

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Roll Up KPIs

### Roll Up KPIs

Group-by + aggregate (like a pivot table) — turns raw rows into KPI numbers.

Next: Write Executive Summary (AI)

### Write Executive Summary (AI)

Single fixed LLM step (no tool choice) — cheaper and more predictable than an agent.

Ports: ①→ Compose HTML Report · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Google Gemini Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `models/gemini-2.5-flash`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Write Executive Summary (AI)** as `languageModel`

🔑 Google AI API key

### Compose HTML Report

Custom JavaScript: Build an HTML digest table from the aggregated rows

Next: Send Report via Telegram

### Send Report via Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

Ports: ①→ Cap Batch Volume · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 100 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Airtable)

### Append Audit Row (Airtable)

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Events' table.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Telegram · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Send Summary to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#alerts`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Compile a daily digest with Airtable and Telegram with instant alerts

**Pattern:** Scheduled Digest · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Compile a daily digest with Airtable and Telegram with instant alerts. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual thresholds is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Gather from Airtable → Blend Sources → Roll Up KPIs → Write Executive Summary (AI) → Compose HTML Report → Send Report via Telegram → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to scheduled digest without manual effort
- Anyone automating thresholds in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Responds in seconds
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; Google AI API key; Slack OAuth2; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

KPIs are rolled up with Summarize (exact group-by + aggregate) before any AI touches the data — asking an LLM to 'figure out the numbers' from raw records is slower, costlier, and less reliable than doing exact arithmetic in n8n and only asking the model to narrate numbers that are already correct.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Add a second data source and watch it blend into the same report via Merge.
2. Toggle the AI executive-summary step on and off and compare the report's usefulness.
3. Change the KPI grouping field and see how the pivot reshapes.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your thresholds process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
